name: Peña Soto, Ermita de NªSra de Villavieja, Nalda – circular route
name_original: Peña Soto, Ermita de NªSra de Villavieja, Nalda – ruta circular
region_intro: Hiking around Nalda offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by hills, rocky outcrops like Peña Soto and Peña Bajenza, and historical hermitages. The landscape features a mix of open areas and paths leading through natural features typical of the La Rioja region in Spain. Elevation changes are present, with trails ascending to viewpoints and mountain passes. Best hiking trails around Nalda The most popular hiking route is Peña Soto, Ermita de NªSra de Villavieja, Nalda – circular route, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This circular path includes views of Peña Soto and passes by the Ermita de NªSra de Villavieja. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cruz de Peña Bajenza – Mountain pass loop from Islallana, a moderate 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This route features a mountain pass loop, offering ascents and panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Peña Soto – San Marcos Hermitage loop from La Rioja, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through areas around Peña Soto and the San Marcos Hermitage, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Nalda is defined by rocky hills, cultural landmarks such as hermitages, and varied paths that include mountain passes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, accommodating different ability levels. The routes in Nalda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Nalda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Nalda?
A: Hiking around Nalda features varied terrain, including hills and rocky outcrops like Peña Soto and Peña Bajenza. You'll find paths leading through natural features typical of the La Rioja region in Spain, often with noticeable elevation changes as trails ascend to viewpoints and mountain passes. Historical hermitages are also common sights along the routes.

Q: Are there hiking options for all skill levels in Nalda?
A: Yes, Nalda offers a range of hiking options suitable for different abilities. The area has 23 routes in total, including 7 easy trails, 14 moderate trails, and 2 more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Nalda?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989418970/pena-soto-ermita-de-nsra-de-villavieja-nalda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Soto, Ermita de NªSra de Villavieja, Nalda – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 miles (10.6 km) long. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Soto – San Marcos Hermitage loop from La Rioja&lt;/a&gt;, covering 5.4 miles (8.7 km).

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Nalda?
A: While hiking, you can encounter prominent rocky formations such as Peña Soto and Peña Bajenza. The region also features historical hermitages. Nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clavijo Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Peñuejos Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any good viewpoints on the trails around Nalda?
A: Yes, many trails offer panoramic views, especially those that ascend to mountain passes or rocky outcrops like Peña Bajenza. You can also find specific viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3753514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leza Canyon Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Nalda?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the presence of cultural landmarks like hermitages, and the well-defined paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for families or beginners in Nalda?
A: Yes, Nalda offers 7 easy hiking trails that are well-suited for families or those new to hiking. These routes typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing a pleasant outdoor experience without excessive challenge.

Q: How long do hikes around Nalda typically take?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989418970/pena-soto-ermita-de-nsra-de-villavieja-nalda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Soto, Ermita de NªSra de Villavieja, Nalda – circular route&lt;/a&gt; takes approximately 3 hours 21 minutes to complete, while the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5328933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Peña Bajenza – Mountain pass loop from Islallana&lt;/a&gt; can be finished in about 2 hours.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to a mountain pass?
A: Yes, you can find trails that include mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5328933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Peña Bajenza – Mountain pass loop from Islallana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.4-mile (5.4 km) path specifically designed as a mountain pass loop, offering ascents and panoramic views.

Q: What is the longest hiking route available in Nalda?
A: Among the listed routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989418970/pena-soto-ermita-de-nsra-de-villavieja-nalda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Soto, Ermita de NªSra de Villavieja, Nalda – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer options, spanning 6.6 miles (10.6 km) and typically taking over 3 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find trails that explore the area around Peña Soto?
A: Yes, Peña Soto is a prominent feature in the Nalda hiking area. You can explore it via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989418970/pena-soto-ermita-de-nsra-de-villavieja-nalda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Soto, Ermita de NªSra de Villavieja, Nalda – circular route&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Soto – San Marcos Hermitage loop from La Rioja&lt;/a&gt;, both offering views and paths around this rocky outcrop.
tips: Views of Peña Soto, imposing from below.
Beautiful hermitage on a small hill and surrounded by grass and trees.
Entrance arch to the walled area of Nalda and access to the castle.

Splendid castle in the process of restoration, it is worth the climb. The date of construction is not clear but it seems to have been in the 13th century. In the 19th century it suffered abandonment and plunder, its stones being used for other constructions in the area.
